key_insight: A self-supervised, mask-aware Transformer can learn force estimation from FBG sensors while explicitly handling missing channels and nonlinear cross-axis coupling.
engineering_relevance: This enables more robust minimally invasive surgical robot sensing under fiber fractures and intermittent channel dropouts without requiring a separate model for every failure pattern.
method: Pretrain with masked-channel reconstruction on unlabeled sensor streams.
Fine-tune for force regression using clean and corrupted views plus a dynamic corruption curriculum.
Add a heteroscedastic uncertainty head to predict per-axis confidence in one forward pass.
evidence_signal: On an 8-channel catheter-scale FBG dataset, the model achieved 0.0066 N RMSE nominally and 0.0126 N with 4-channel failures, outperforming a 255-model bank (0.0154 N under 4-channel loss).
watch_item: Performance depends on representative masked/corrupted training data, so unusual failure modes or sensor geometries may still require re-training or adaptation.
paper_title: Self-Supervised Mask-Aware Transformers for Fault-Tolerant FBG Force Sensing in Minimally Invasive Surgical Robotics
